title: Cherai Beach
tagline: Cherai Beach — beach town of Kerala

overview.short: Cherai Beach is located in Cherai on the northern side of Vypin Island, a suburb of the city of Kochi in the state of Kerala, India.
overview.description: Cherai Beach is located in Cherai on the northern side of Vypin Island, a suburb of the city of Kochi in the state of Kerala, India. One of the most visited beaches in the state, it is situated around 25 km (15 mi) from downtown Kochi and 20 km (12 mi) from Cochin International Airport.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Chakkarakadavu
topPlaces.description: Chakkarakadavu is a small village to the east of the town of Cherai, on Vypin Island in Ernakulam district, Kerala, India. Chakkarakadavu shares a common history with Cherai and with the greater Vypin Island.

topPlaces.name: Cherai Gowreeshwara Temple
topPlaces.description: The Cherai Gowreeshwara Temple is a Hindu temple in the village of Cherai on Vypeen island in the state of Kerala in South India.

topPlaces.name: Pallippuram, Ernakulam
topPlaces.description: Pallippuram (also known in English as Palliport or in Portuguese as Paliporte) is a village on Vypeen island, in Kochi, Kerala, south India. The village is located approximately 25 km from Ernakulam and 20 km from Cochin International Airport.

topPlaces.name: Kuriapilly
topPlaces.description: Kuriappilly now known as Kuriappilly, Kuriappilly South, labor Junction, Shappupadi (Toddy parlor ferry, Kappelapadi. Kuriapilly was a border of Travancore kingdom in the British India, the name Kuriapilly is old up to 500 years, even in the letters of Tippu Sultan we can see the name Kuriapilly.

topPlaces.name: Madaplathuruth
topPlaces.description: Madaplathuruth is a village in Paravur Taluk, state of Kerala. Formerly known as Kuriapilly, it was a part of the Travancore kingdom in the British India.

topPlaces.name: Basilica of Our Lady of Snows, Pallippuram
topPlaces.description: The Basilica of Our Lady of Snows, Pallippuram, Ernakulam, Kerala, is a minor basilica. The basilica decree was issued on 27 August 2012.

image.alt: Basilica of Our Lady of Snows, Pallippuram

topPlaces.name: Pallipuram Fort
topPlaces.description: Pallippuram Fort or (Paleport Castelo em Cima) is a fort in Pallippuram, Vyppin, Ernakulam district of Kerala, south India. It was built by Portuguese sailors on 27 September, 1503 using just timber wood, and later renovated in 1505 by replacing timber structure with stone.

topPlaces.name: Pattanam
topPlaces.description: Pattanam (Malayalam: [pɐʈːɐɳɐm]) is a village located in Paravur taluk, Ernakulam district, in the southern Indian state of Kerala. It is situated 2–3 km northwest of North Paravur, 3 km west of Chendamangalam, and 30 km north of Kochi (Cochin).

items.note: Chakkarakadavu is a small village to the east of the town of Cherai, on Vypin Island in Ernakulam district, Kerala, India. Chakkarakadavu shares a common history with Cherai and with the greater Vypin Island.
The Cherai Gowreeshwara Temple is a Hindu temple in the village of Cherai on Vypeen island in the state of Kerala in South India.

Pallippuram (also known in English as Palliport or in Portuguese as Paliporte) is a village on Vypeen island, in Kochi, Kerala, south India. The village is located approximately 25 km from Ernakulam and 20 km from Cochin International Airport.

Kuriappilly now known as Kuriappilly, Kuriappilly South, labor Junction, Shappupadi (Toddy parlor ferry, Kappelapadi. Kuriapilly was a border of Travancore kingdom in the British India, the name Kuriapilly is old up to 500 years, even in the letters of Tippu Sultan we can see the name Kuriapilly.
Madaplathuruth is a village in Paravur Taluk, state of Kerala. Formerly known as Kuriapilly, it was a part of the Travancore kingdom in the British India.

items.activity: Basilica of Our Lady of Snows, Pallippuram
items.note: The Basilica of Our Lady of Snows, Pallippuram, Ernakulam, Kerala, is a minor basilica. The basilica decree was issued on 27 August 2012.

Pallippuram Fort or (Paleport Castelo em Cima) is a fort in Pallippuram, Vyppin, Ernakulam district of Kerala, south India. It was built by Portuguese sailors on 27 September, 1503 using just timber wood, and later renovated in 1505 by replacing timber structure with stone.

Pattanam (Malayalam: [pɐʈːɐɳɐm]) is a village located in Paravur taluk, Ernakulam district, in the southern Indian state of Kerala. It is situated 2–3 km northwest of North Paravur, 3 km west of Chendamangalam, and 30 km north of Kochi (Cochin).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Cherai Beach?
faq.a: The best time to visit Cherai Beach is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Cherai Beach?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (25 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (23 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Cherai Beach?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Cherai Beach at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Cherai Beach?
faq.a: Stays in Cherai Beach start from ₹1,200 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Cherai Beach?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chakkarakadavu, Cherai Gowreeshwara Temple, Pallippuram, Ernakulam, Kuriapilly, Madaplathuruth.
